privileged_info_summary: The task asks the agent to create a 3D model of a shaft collar from scratch, thus the data is purely synthetic. The expected output file paths (/home/ga/Documents/SolveSpace/radial_collar.slvs and .stl) match the bash export scripts perfectly. The claimed geometric volumes (no_cut: 6361, target single cut: 5985, double cut: 5607) were mathematically verified by computing the analytic intersection integrals of a 15mm-radius hollow cylinder (7.5mm inner radius, 12mm height) and a 4mm-radius orthogonal hole cylinder. The slight deviations from theoretical continuous volumes (e.g. 5985 vs 5977.5 mm^3) correspond exactly to SolveSpace's inscribed polygon mesh tessellation leaving more solid behind during boolean difference operations. A volume tolerance between 5700 and 6200 robustly isolates the correct one-sided cut state.
